summary: I re-traced the iteration-3 technical design against all 34 business requirements (R1-R34), grounded in the discovery notes' account of what the existing Define UI actually surfaces, and confirmed the two coverage gaps from iteration 2 are now genuinely and specifically closed. F1 (major, R19): a completed 'Repeat from the Beginning' restart regenerates business_requirements — Define's headline deliverable — in addition to discovery_notes and open_questions; the design now surfaces it inline via a new define_business_requirements helper (§6.2), a new 'Requirements' block rendered in DOM order before the paused menu (§6.3), and a rewritten R19 traceability row, so the person sees all three fresh, replaced artifacts before choosing Done. F2 (minor, R13/R14): the Ask Human answer block's condition is widened from questions.present? to questions.present? || phase.paused? with an empty-state message and relabeled notes field (§2.9, §6.3), so the free-form-notes-as-feedback path is reachable even when a paused Define has no open questions yet — leaning on the fact that AnswerQuestions never required questions to exist. Every one of the 34 requirements, including the newly-added R12 (in-flight single-step re-run hides the menu and shows a live indicator, covered by menu_busy), maps to a concrete, cited design element in §7's traceability table and the underlying sections. Existing-behavior requirements R32-R34 are satisfied by the design's strictly-additive approach (new status value + two booleans; no existing status, allow-list entry, or gate path removed). No meaningful gold-plating exists: the only unrequested elements are the unused pause_requested_at column and the unpersisted user: params, both trivial, additive, and self-acknowledged as future-audit hooks; the discovery_notes and business_requirements inline surfacing is demanded by R9 and R19 respectively, not speculative. The design is complete and appropriately scoped.
